items.title: Jay-Z, Yankee Stadium, And How New York Grew Up With The Blueprint
items.summary:
Toward the beginning of last Saturday’s brief rap god reunion, a noble Black woman once again saved me from myself. Or rather, from about nine shots of Espolon and a surge of fanboy euphoria. Moments beforehand, Eminem had emerged from the dark shadows of a Yankee Stadium tunnel to join Jay-Z to perform “Renegade” at HOV’s Blueprint 25th anniversary concert, and I'd unsheathed my iPhone 15 and started filming the moment. Or at least I thought. My dumbass rapped along to Slim Shady’s verse for about 13 seconds before she tapped me on the shoulder with an important mission directive: “Press record.”

The post Jay-Z, Yankee Stadium, And How New York Grew Up With <em>The Blueprint</em> appeared first on Stereogum.

items.title: The 5 Best Songs Of The Week
items.summary:
Every week the Stereogum staff chooses the five best new songs of the week. The eligibility period begins and ends Thursdays right before midnight. You can hear this week’s picks below and on Stereogum’s Favorite New Music Spotify playlist, which is updated weekly. (An expanded playlist of our new music picks is available to subscribers on Spotify and Apple Music, updated throughout the week.)

The post The 5 Best Songs Of The Week appeared first on Stereogum.

items.title: Fenne Lily – “Thank You Sorry”
items.summary:
You like really intense lyrics about post-breakup epiphanies? Well, here's one for you: "You thank me for a good year and go/ And you say it like you have to leave/ You're sorry for the good parts, as well as what you really did wrong/ But it's all the same to me." Those are the opening lines from the British-born, Brooklyn-based singer-songwriter Fenne Lily's new song "Thank You Sorry." She gets right to it!

The post Fenne Lily – “Thank You Sorry” appeared first on Stereogum.

items.title: Hopscotch Fest Announces 2026 Lineup
items.summary:
Every September, the Hopscotch Music Festival brings an eclectic, idiosyncratic lineup to venues all around Raleigh. It's a great time. The whole Stereogum staff went down to the fest in 2019, and we saw a ton of cool stuff. Last year's fest looked sick, with a bill that include Earl Sweatshirt, Sparks, Godspeed You Black Emperor!, Geese, and Unwound. Today, the Hopscotch organizers announced the lineup for this year's fest, which goes down Sep. 10-12, and it's got some surprises.

The post Hopscotch Fest Announces 2026 Lineup appeared first on Stereogum.

items.title: Hear Bright Eyes & The Lumineers’ Tracks From A New Felice Brothers Tribute Album
items.summary:
New York folk-rock ramblers the Felice Brothers started out busking in subway stations more than two decades ago, and now they're getting their own tribute album. Brandy Schultz, Felice Brothers supporter and wife of Lumineers frontman Wesley Schultz, put the tribute LP together Felice County Fair, and it's got Felice Brothers covers from people like Brandon Flowers, Dawes, Old Crow Medicine Show, Deer Tick, Nathaniel Rateliff, and Bebe Stockwell.

The post Hear Bright Eyes & The Lumineers’ Tracks From A New Felice Brothers Tribute Album appeared first on Stereogum.

items.title: The Olivia Tremor Control Announce First New Album In 27 Years The Same Place
items.summary:
The idea of a new Olivia Tremor Control was basically unthinkable, but it's happening anyway. The lo-fi psychedelic pop band was a key driving force in Athens, Georgia's Elephant 6 collective in the '90s, but they only released two cult-beloved albums, 1996's Dusk At Cubist Castle and 1999's Black Foliage: Animation Music Volume One, before breaking up. Years later, the band got together to play reunion shows, and co-leaders Bill Doss and Will Cullen Hart both passed away — Doss in 2012, Hart in 2024. But Doss and Hart's bandmates have finished work on the massive double album that the two of them started work on 20 years ago, and now it's coming out.

The post The Olivia Tremor Control Announce First New Album In 27 Years <em>The Same Place</em> appeared first on Stereogum.

items.title: Generacion Suicida Are Here To Depress The Punks
items.summary:
"We're here to depress the punks, I guess." That was Tony Abarca, frontman of the LA band Generacion Suicida, talking to a restaurant patio full of punks in Richmond on Wednesday night. During one of his infrequent moments of between-songs patter, Abarca admitted to being "not much of a talker," but "depress the punks, I guess" is a pretty accurate summary of Generacion Suicida's mission. They're definitely a punk band. Their fans are punks. (I'm taking a moment here to acknowledge that I skipped this column last month and that this month's installment is about a punk show, not a hardcore show. It's OK. We'll get through it together.) But Generacion Suicida's version of punk is relatively slow and atmospheric, full of heavy sustain and gothed-out guitar-twinkles. The only problem is that you can't really get too depressed while listening to Generacion Suicida. They're too good for that.

The post Generacion Suicida Are Here To Depress The Punks appeared first on Stereogum.

items.title: Blackberry Crush – “Patterns”
items.summary:
Spacey-gauzy alt-rock band Blackberry Crush started off as a duo in Wyoming before moving to Denver and expanding into a four-piece. They make woozy, riffy music that's at least shoegaze-adjacent. But they're more direct than most shoegaze bands, and their sighing harmony vocals set them apart.

The post Blackberry Crush – “Patterns” appeared first on Stereogum.

items.title: Matt Berninger – “Martini Me Fatso”
items.summary:
Publicly, Matt Berninger's been in solo mode for well over a year now between the rollout and the touring for his album Get Sunk. It seems he is not ready to shift out of that gear yet. The National frontman has a new solo single out today with an extremely memorable title.

The post Matt Berninger – “Martini Me Fatso” appeared first on Stereogum.

items.title: Mike D 5D – “Crypto”
items.summary:
I'll admit: The song title had me worried. As in, what would we do if we found out that either of the surviving Beastie Boys was really into crypto? Or if they owned companies that built AI data centers or some shit like that? Doesn't that at least seem plausible? Mike D is obviously a cool guy, but he's also old and rich. With plenty of our aging gen-X icons, we're starting to see the "old and rich" tendencies win out over the "cool" ones. It fucking sucks. Thankfully, though, this is not one of those cases. Mike D has a new song that is both called "Crypto" and about crypto, and I'm happy to tell you that it's about how crypto is bad.

The post Mike D 5D – “Crypto” appeared first on Stereogum.

items.title: IFULKI – “Something Greater”
items.summary:
The debut single from London group IFULKI is a compelling introduction. The experimental group, which draws influence from Gnawa, electronic, and jazz music, delivers a rich first offering with their song "Something Greater." It's a collage of ominous trip-hop textures, soulful piano embellishments, anxious percussion, and soaring vocals.

The post IFULKI – “Something Greater” appeared first on Stereogum.

items.title: Homework Announce Debut Album Bang Here If Stuck: Hear “Made”
items.summary:
I would love to understand the rationale for picking "Homework" as a band name. That's what a young Glasgow indie band did, and I just want to know what it means. Did they intentionally pick a name that you can't really Google and that generally has negative connotations among basically all the people who ever had to go to school? Did they just name themselves after the Daft Punk album, despite their music offering no indication that they've ever even heard the Daft Punk album? Also, do people even use the word "homework" in Scotland, or do they call it "revising" or something? So many questions! I'm just a curious guy!

The post Homework Announce Debut Album <em>Bang Here If Stuck</em>: Hear “Made” appeared first on Stereogum.

items.title: Mick Jagger Talks Olivia Rodrigo, AI Music
items.summary:
The Rolling Stones are the latest cover stars for Billboard. Last week, the Stones released their 27th studio album Foreign Tongues, which my colleague Chris dubbed "a fascinating listen." Now, in separate interviews, Mick Jagger and Keith Richards shared insight on the new album, Olivia Rodrigo, AI, and more.

The post Mick Jagger Talks Olivia Rodrigo, AI Music appeared first on Stereogum.

items.title: Clairo Joins Ryan Beatty For Live Debut Of Their New Collabs In NYC
items.summary:
The first time that Clairo ever performed her hit "Sexy To Someone" wasn't at one of her own shows. Instead, back in 2024 she made a surprise appearance during a set from Ryan Beatty, her peer in chilled-out singer-songwriterdom. Last month, Clairo sang backup on a few songs from Beatty's new album Sweet Fortune. And on Thursday night, Clairo once again took the stage alongside Beatty, performing two songs together for the first time in New York.

The post Clairo Joins Ryan Beatty For Live Debut Of Their New Collabs In NYC appeared first on Stereogum.

items.title: Lorde Continues AI Criticism By Calling Out New Spotify Feature
items.summary:
It's always a great day when your favorite pop stars call out rubbish technology, and Lorde is on a roll. Last week, Lorde voiced her disdain for the "fucked up" Ray-Ban Meta AI Glasses at Madrid's Mad Cool Festival. Now, she's coming for Spotify's new About The Song feature that summarizes a song's context and spews out incorrect facts in order to make the art seem more compelling, thus tricking you into spending more time on the app. Yesterday, she posted about the inaccurate info that accompanies her song "Current Affairs."

The post Lorde Continues AI Criticism By Calling Out New Spotify Feature appeared first on Stereogum.

items.title: James Blake, Travis Scott, & Ludwig Göransson – “When I’m Home”
items.summary:
Christopher Nolan's The Odyssey, one of the most anticipated films of 2026, is out today. The epic tale called for an equally epic cast, which includes Matt Damon, Tom Holland, Anne Hathaway, Zendaya, Lupita Nyong'o, Charlize Theron, Jon Bernthal, and even Travis Scott. Scott is also on the movie's original soundtrack, which is available today as well. He contributed to the closing track "When I'm Home," alongside his longtime collaborator James Blake and Oscar-winning composer Ludwig Göransson.

The post James Blake, Travis Scott, & Ludwig Göransson – “When I’m Home” appeared first on Stereogum.

items.title: Brandon Flowers – “Paradise”
items.summary:
For his first solo album in 11 years, Brandon Flowers went country. The Killers frontman announced Thrasher in June, revealing that he'd decamped to Nashville with producers Shawn Everett and Jonathan Rado, both of whom worked on the most recent Killers records. Not long after, he unveiled the new album's lead single, "Plans." Today, he's released a second advance track.

The post Brandon Flowers – “Paradise” appeared first on Stereogum.

items.title: MSG Suing Wired Over Article Alleging Company Kept Database Of LGBTQIA Celebrities
items.summary:
A week ago, Wired published a report alleging Madison Square Garden Entertainment Corp. maintains a database of LGBTQIA celebrities. At the time, MSG said they'd be suing Wired over the article. As promised, that lawsuit was filed today.

The post MSG Suing <em>Wired</em> Over Article Alleging Company Kept Database Of LGBTQIA Celebrities appeared first on Stereogum.

items.title: The Cure’s Robert Smith Blasts FIFA Over Trump Presenting World Cup Trophy
items.summary:
This year's FIFA Men's World Cup took place in the United States, Canada, and Mexico, and it's been Americanized to an unprecedented extent. No, I'm not talking about Donald Trump's meddling with Folarin Balogun's red card suspension; FIFA was already quite corrupt before our corrupt president got involved. But I am referring to the hydration breaks during each half that somehow became ad breaks. And then there's the halftime show.

The post The Cure’s Robert Smith Blasts FIFA Over Trump Presenting World Cup Trophy appeared first on Stereogum.

items.title: Hana Stretton – “Seagull Theory II” & “As It Was Before This”
items.summary:
The British-Australian musical artist Hana Stretton specializes in ambient folk, seemingly merging samples, field recordings, and original tracks into soft smears of emotion. Her songs inspire warm comparisons to geniuses like Grouper and Claire Rousay, and she's gained a fervent supporter in Phil Elverum, who reissued her 2023 album Soon on his label P.W. Elverum & Suns. Last month, Stretton announced her next LP, tiarn, and shared two powerful new tracks. Today we get two more.

The post Hana Stretton – “Seagull Theory II” & “As It Was Before This” appeared first on Stereogum.

items.title: Petey USA – “Save For The Horses”
items.summary:
Back in May, Petey USA continued to explore his mix of emo, heartland rock, and electronics on the new single "Kiss The City." He's since given us the bummed-out synth-pop slow-build "Like A Mantra," and now there's a third new self-produced track.

The post Petey USA – “Save For The Horses” appeared first on Stereogum.

items.title: Lava La Rue – “Prince Of Bologna”
items.summary:
The latest artist to contribute to Speedy Wunderground’s singles series is London's Lava La Rue. For the new single "Prince Of Bologna," Lava La Rue was influenced by fragments of conversation overheard at the smoking area of a London venue. Taken out of context, these shards of convo become uniquely iridescent, beautiful and batshit crazy. The track was written and co-produced with Tomasso Medica (Husky Loops).

The post Lava La Rue – “Prince Of Bologna” appeared first on Stereogum.

items.title: Tinashe Announces New Album Popstar: Hear “Crash Out”
items.summary:
Not that long ago, Tinashe was a cult-beloved singer who was stuck in seemingly endless major-label purgatory, despite obvious talent and charisma, as well as a pretty decent little run of hits. Ever since she finally went independent a few years ago, she's been thriving. She scored a couple of excellent viral hits on her 2024 album, 2024's Quantum Baby, and now she's following it with another one called Popstar. She has earned that title.

The post Tinashe Announces New Album <em>Popstar</em>: Hear “Crash Out” appeared first on Stereogum.

items.title: Carly Rae Jepsen – “After All”
items.summary:
Around the time that this summer draws to an end, cult-pop overlord Carly Rae Jepsen will return with Day And Night, a new 24-song double album divided into two distinct and self-explanatory halves. Jepsen has already shared lead single "On Wires," which comes from the daytime half of the record. Now, she's got another new one, and it's also from the day disc. She's really leaning into the sunniness right now.

The post Carly Rae Jepsen – “After All” appeared first on Stereogum.

items.title: Hovvdy – “You Will Go Far”
items.summary:
Texas duo Will Taylor and Charlie Martin, aka Hovvdy, don't make nostalgic music, but there's a sentimental time-shiftiness that runs through their songwriting. Their latest singles, "Blast" and "Try Try Try," from the forthcoming album Big World, are especially adept at keeping the atmosphere light and groovy even as they tussle with weightier emotions. Their newest single "You Will Go Far" is no different.

The post Hovvdy – “You Will Go Far” appeared first on Stereogum.

items.title: No Joy & Fire-Tooz – “Barking At The Sun”
items.summary:
In 1983, Ozzy Osbourne released Bark At The Moon, the album where he's on the cover looking like a werewolf. That was awesome, and you couldn't ask for a better twist on the whole Ozzy thing. Yesterday, the Montreal fuzz-pop auteur No Joy dropped a new track called "Barking At The Sun." I don't think she was necessarily trying to flip that Ozzy title, but the inversion still makes sense, at least to me.

The post No Joy & Fire-Tooz – “Barking At The Sun” appeared first on Stereogum.

items.title: silt. – “trauma porn”
items.summary:
The press release for newly formed creative group silt. is elusive. They "exist between London and the inherited landscapes of the Lake District and Norfolk" and are not "a traditional band nor solo alias." Hell, there aren't even any names listed. But the fact remains, their new single "trauma porn" is eerie and gripping and intriguing.

The post silt. – “trauma porn” appeared first on Stereogum.

items.title: Ceremony – “Dark Summer”
items.summary:
See the photo credit up there? "Martin Sorrondeguy" — the same Martin Sorrondeguy, presumably, from Los Crudos and Limp Wrist. (I don't think there's two Martin Sorrondeguys.) That means that Ceremony got a DIY punk legend to take their press pictures. That's fucking sick. It also makes perfect sense, since Ceremony are DIY punk legends themselves.

The post Ceremony – “Dark Summer” appeared first on Stereogum.

items.title: Envie – “Violently Friendly”
items.summary:
The members of the East London trio Envie (pronounced on-vee) have been friends since school. They've been at it for a while — their Bandcamp page under former band name Dystopia was last active in 2020, and they featured on FKA twigs' Caprisongs track "Which Way" — but it looks like they're ready to reintroduce themselves. The first upload on a fresh YouTube account is a practice-space recording of "Violently Friendly," a rock song with a bright, melodic immediacy.

The post Envie – “Violently Friendly” appeared first on Stereogum.

items.title: Perennial – “Modernism”
items.summary:
Well, shake it up baby, now. Twist and shout. Come on, come on, come on, come on baby, now. Come on and work it on out. As usual, that old-school rock 'n' roll spirit courses through Perennial's new single "Modernism," and so does bracing party-punk sass. The title track from the New England trio's new album has a lot going on despite its spry, minimal arrangement. Instruments enter and exit the frame all the time as Chelsey Hahn and Chad Jewitt trade off rallying cries over a pogo-punk beat. Both the craftsmanship and the energy level are remarkable.

The post Perennial – “Modernism” appeared first on Stereogum.

items.title: Standing On The Corner Announce New Album SOTC (“II”) Feat. Solange, Danny Brown, Styles P, More: Hear Two Songs
items.summary:
The mysterious Brooklyn collective Standing On The Corner released a new album last year, but you probably didn't hear it. You had to really work to hear SOTC (“II”), since the LP has never appeared on a streaming service or anything. Instead, it was only available as a $50 DVD that you could buy from some guy with a bag full of them in Times Square on 9/11. That was it. It was the only way. But that's about to change, since SOTC (“II”) will now get a proper release this year on 9/11, exactly one year after that sketchy DVD sale.

The post Standing On The Corner Announce New Album <em>SOTC (“II”)</em> Feat. Solange, Danny Brown, Styles P, More: Hear Two Songs appeared first on Stereogum.

items.title: GB – “The Next Day”
items.summary:
I'd tell you to remember the name Gustav Berntsen, but the man records as GB, so initials will suffice. The Copenhagen-based musical artist is ramping up to the release of Herzsprung, his new album for AD 93, and he's got another stellar track out this week. "The Next Day" continues Berntsen's tradition of elegant, sophisticated guitar-pop. The cello and piano class this song up quite a bit, but the guitar and vocals at its core would be magnetic under any circumstances. Listen below.

The post GB – “The Next Day” appeared first on Stereogum.

items.title: CD Sales Growth Outpaces Vinyl (Despite Half Of Gen Z & Millennial CD Buyers Not Owning CD Players)
items.summary:
Wednesday, entertainment and music data compiler Luminate released their midyear report that "highlights changing consumer behaviors and the evolving economics driving music, television & film." Although data really bores me, there's some interesting things happening in this new report, including the news that CD sales are on a massive rise despite only half of Gen Z and millennials actually owning a CD player.

The post CD Sales Growth Outpaces Vinyl (Despite Half Of Gen Z & Millennial CD Buyers Not Owning CD Players) appeared first on Stereogum.

items.title: Hank Heaven Announces New EP Welterweight: Hear “I Wanna Be Your Man”
items.summary:
Hey, here's a good lyric: "I got my own place but got no rent to pay 'cause it turns out the landlord is in prison/ But still when it’s late, I’ll take the B48 down to your house like I’m on a mission." I love that kind of specificity. That line comes from "I Wanna Be Your Man," the latest single from the craggy-voiced New York singer-songwriter Hank Heaven. They sing it with conversational ease over a spare acoustic guitar and some slow-swelling strings and woodwinds. I hear a nice contrast between the stately arrangement and Heaven's dazed drawl.

The post Hank Heaven Announces New EP <em>Welterweight</em>: Hear “I Wanna Be Your Man” appeared first on Stereogum.

items.title: Man/Woman/Chainsaw – “Something Else To Give”
items.summary:
We named Man/Woman/Chainsaw a Band To Watch two years ago, and the singles from their debut album Cannonball have been validating that choice. The young London group plays artsy, orchestrally infused alt-rock — not unlike their forebears Black Country, New Road, but with less theater-kid energy and more of a pleasing radio-friendly edge. This week they've added one last single to a list that already includes "Only Girl," "Nosedive," "Get Up And Dance," and "Goddamn, Lizard Man!"

The post Man/Woman/Chainsaw – “Something Else To Give” appeared first on Stereogum.

items.title: Pink Eye Club – “Small Talk”
items.summary:
The latest single from Pink Eye Club, Windmill scene mainstay and disco luminary Haydn Davies, reminded me that I have an unfinished podcast I must listen to. The other day I started listening to Dr. Laurie Santos' latest hot take on her podcast The Happiness Lab, which eloquently broke down why small talk sucks. Now Davies' new track — which previews his forthcoming album Emotional Dance Music, due out in October — made a spunky soundtrack for this opinion.

The post Pink Eye Club – “Small Talk” appeared first on Stereogum.

items.title: Echo & The Bunnymen Announce First New Album In 12 Years Apples For Isaac: Hear “Brussels Is Haunted”
items.summary:
It has been 46 years since Liverpool lads Echo & The Bunnymen started making theatrically bummed-out psychedelic goth-pop anthems, and they aren't done quite yet. For half a decade, Echo & The Bunnymen have been talking up a new studio album, their first record of new material since 2014's Meteorites. (They also dropped a collection of re-recordings called The Stars, The Oceans & The Moon in 2018, but that's not what we're talking about here.) Now, they're finally ready to announce the impending arrival of that album, which is called Apples For Isaac.

The post Echo & The Bunnymen Announce First New Album In 12 Years <em>Apples For Isaac</em>: Hear “Brussels Is Haunted” appeared first on Stereogum.

items.title: Lily Allen Downsizes US Tour Venues
items.summary:
This spring, Lily Allen brought her tour supporting West End Girl, her recent concept album about the breakdown of her marriage, to theaters across America. She was set to bring the show stateside again in September, this time in arenas. That plan has since been adjusted.

The post Lily Allen Downsizes US Tour Venues appeared first on Stereogum.

items.title: Wanna Feel Old? This Is Phoebe Bridgers’ Tonight Show Band Now
items.summary:
On Tuesday, Phoebe Bridgers was a surprise guest on Jimmy Fallon's Tonight Show. Her backing band was a group of very skilled kids, nodding to the "Lost Boys" she sang about in her Peter Pan-inspired comeback single. Yesterday, Bridgers returned to Fallon to perform an acoustic version of "Lost Boys," the first single from her forthcoming album Lost Weekend. It may have only been a day, but the chorus' "never grow up" premise no longer seemed to apply to her backing band.

The post Wanna Feel Old? This Is Phoebe Bridgers’ <em>Tonight Show</em> Band Now appeared first on Stereogum.

items.title: Lana Del Rey Shares Update On Stove… And Companion Album?!
items.summary:
Lana Del Rey has been frequently teasing and pushing back her follow-up to 2023's Did You Know That There's a Tunnel Under Ocean Blvd for more than two years now. When she first announced it at a Grammys party in 2024, it was called Lasso and billed as a country album. Since then its title has changed at least twice — first to The Right Person Will Stay, then to Stove — and Del Rey has clarified that it won't be a drastic departure from her usual Americana-tinted sound.

The post Lana Del Rey Shares Update On <em>Stove</em>… And Companion Album?! appeared first on Stereogum.
